persona.role: Senior Data Engineer specializing in Analytics Dashboards
persona.expertise: PostgreSQL Analytics, Recharts, Next.js, TypeScript, Data Visualization
persona.communication_style: Technical, with focus on actionable metrics

task.objective: Add failure rate metrics and trend visualization to the dashboard
task.success_criteria: - Calculate failure rate as core metric (failed / total * 100) - Display failure rate in stats cards with color-coded severity - Show failure rate trend chart over time - Track failure volume (absolute count) - Provide average reference line in chart - Integrate with existing date filters

context.background: Failure rate is a key reliability metric that complements pass rate. While pass rate focuses on success, failure rate helps identify: - Degradation trends before they become critical - Impact of code changes on test reliability - Areas needing attention
context.constraints: - No database migrations required (calculated from existing data) - Use Recharts for visualization - Must work with existing date filters - Color-coded thresholds for quick assessment

instructions.step: Update DashboardMetrics interface to include failure_rate and failure_volume
Update getDashboardMetrics query to calculate failure_rate
Update GET /api/trends to support type=failures parameter
Update StatsCards to display failure rate card
Add average reference line to chart

always.rule: Use red color theme for failure metrics

never.rule: Never show more than 1 decimal place
Never divide by zero (handle empty data)

output_format.structure: 1. Updated type definitions 2. Updated database functions 3. Updated API route 4. Updated StatsCards component 5. New FailureRateChart component 6. Dashboard integration

metric.name: failure_volume
metric.description: Absolute number of failed test runs

features.feature: Line chart showing failure rate over time
Red color theme for failure data
Tooltip showing date, rate, and counts

sql_queries.query: WITH execution_stats AS ( SELECT COUNT(*) as total_executions, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'success') as successful, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'failure') as failed, AVG(duration_ms) as avg_duration FROM test_executions WHERE (${dateFrom}::text IS NULL OR started_at >= ${dateFrom}::timestamptz) AND (${dateTo}::text IS NULL OR started_at <= ${dateTo}::timestamptz) ), recent_stats AS ( SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'failure' AND started_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days') as critical_failures, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE started_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours') as last_24h_executions FROM test_executions ) SELECT es.total_executions, CASE WHEN es.total_executions > 0 THEN ROUND(es.successful::decimal / es.total_executions * 100, 1) ELSE 0 END as pass_rate, CASE WHEN es.total_executions > 0 THEN ROUND(es.failed::decimal / es.total_executions * 100, 1) ELSE 0 END as failure_rate, COALESCE(ROUND(es.avg_duration), 0) as avg_duration_ms, rs.critical_failures, rs.last_24h_executions, es.failed as failure_volume FROM execution_stats es, recent_stats rs

SELECT DATE(started_at) as date, COUNT(*) as total_tests, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'failure') as failed_tests, CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN ROUND(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'failure')::decimal / COUNT(*) * 100, 2) ELSE 0 END as failure_rate FROM test_executions WHERE status != 'running' AND ( CASE WHEN ${dateFrom}::text IS NOT NULL AND ${dateTo}::text IS NOT NULL THEN started_at >= ${dateFrom}::timestamptz AND started_at <= ${dateTo}::timestamptz ELSE started_at >= NOW() - (${days} || ' days')::interval END ) GROUP BY DATE(started_at) ORDER BY date ASC

implementation_checklist.item: [ ] Update GET /api/trends route with type parameter
[ ] Update StatsCards with failure rate card
[ ] Add average reference line to chart
